challenges.title: Custom Exception
challenges.description:
Create an exception class `ValidationError` that inherits from ValueError.

Its __init__ should accept two parameters: `field` and `message`. Store both as instance attributes.

Its __str__ method should return the string "{field}: {message}".
challenges.test_code:
e = ValidationError("email", "invalid format")
assert e.field == "email", f"Expected field='email', got '{e.field}'"
assert e.message == "invalid format", f"Expected message='invalid format', got '{e.message}'"
assert str(e) == "email: invalid format", f"Expected 'email: invalid format', got '{str(e)}'"
assert isinstance(e, ValueError), "ValidationError should inherit from ValueError"
print("All tests passed!")
hints: Store field and message with self.field = field and self.message = message.

challenges.title: Exception Hierarchy
challenges.description:
Create a base exception class `AppError` that inherits from Exception.

Create two subclasses:
- `NotFoundError(AppError)` — takes a `resource` parameter, stores it as an attribute
- `AccessDeniedError(AppError)` — takes a `resource` parameter, stores it as an attribute

Write a function `check_access(resource, exists, allowed)` that:
- Raises NotFoundError(resource) if exists is False
- Raises AccessDeniedError(resource) if allowed is False
- Returns True if both exist and allowed
challenges.test_code:
assert check_access("file.txt", True, True) is True, "Should return True when exists and allowed"
try:
    check_access("x", False, True)
    assert False, "Should have raised NotFoundError"
except NotFoundError as e:
    assert e.resource == "x", f"Expected resource='x', got '{e.resource}'"
    assert isinstance(e, AppError), "NotFoundError should inherit from AppError"
print("All tests passed!")
hints: In each subclass __init__, store self.resource = resource and call super().__init__(resource).
In check_access, check exists first (before allowed) — order matters.
Use 'raise NotFoundError(resource)' to raise with the resource argument.
